objective: Replay a synthetic authorized K-12 family messaging assessment where a teacher can send guardian broadcasts to another class by changing the class id.
assumptions: The lab uses synthetic evidence only.
No real guardian messaging provider is contacted.
Classes, teachers, guardians, and messages are intentionally fake.

phases.objective: Map messaging authorization boundaries and class ownership.
actions: List the synthetic teacher's assigned classes
Review the family messaging policy endpoint
Record the target class outbox before broadcast
successCriteria: Teacher scope, policy, and baseline outbox evidence are recorded
noSubagents: student and guardian impact interpretation stays with the primary operator

phases.objective: Validate client-supplied class id can target another class.
actions: Send a synthetic family broadcast as teacher-north to south-algebra
Confirm the API accepts the cross-class broadcast
Fetch the south-algebra outbox to prove guardian message delivery
successCriteria: A report-ready finding cites policy, broadcast acceptance, and outbox evidence
noSubagents: final family-communications risk wording stays with the primary operator

evidence.title: Synthetic family messaging policy response
evidence.summary: The messaging policy trusts the client-supplied class id and does not enforce teacher ownership.

evidence.title: Synthetic cross-class family broadcast accepted
evidence.summary: teacher-north successfully sent a guardian broadcast to south-algebra, a class outside their assignment.

evidence.title: Synthetic south-algebra guardian outbox after broadcast
evidence.summary: The south-algebra guardian outbox contains the message sent by the unauthorized teacher.

findings.title: Family messaging accepts cross-class guardian broadcasts
findings.description: The synthetic family messaging endpoint authorizes broadcasts based on a client-supplied class id without verifying that the teacher owns the target class.
findings.impact: A teacher or compromised teacher session could send messages to guardians outside their assigned class, causing privacy, safety, and trust issues in school communications.
findings.remediation: Authorize family broadcasts server-side against teacher-class assignments, ignore client ownership hints, log cross-scope attempts, and require elevated approval for school-wide or cross-class sends.
